[Contributors 2](https://github.com/danicat/testquery/graphs/contributors). *   [Go 95.9%](https://github.com/danicat/testquery/search?l=go). *   [Makefile 4.1%](https://github.com/danicat/testquery/search?l=makefile). *   [Contact](https://support.github.com/?tags=dotcom-footer).", "score": 0.71040785, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://forum.inductiveautomation.com/t/select-1-spam-from-a-sql-database-connection/29779", "title": "'SELECT 1' Spam from a SQL database connection - Ignition", "content": "I went through the settings of that connection with our database manager and the only obvious source of that was the \u201cTest on Borrow\u201d Setting, which was on, by default. As far as I know, \u2018SELECT 1\u2019 is the query repetitively used by an Ignition database connection to verify the connection is valid. The rate those validation queries are issued is governed by the validation timeout setting. And, as it is happening across all of them seems to be due to a default setting in how Ignition makes connections, not related to read/write or whether a query is even desired. The test-on-borrow setting, which defaults to true, causes every query elsewhere in Ignition to be preceded by a validation query. If you put the logger `gateway.Database.ConnectionManager.FaultedRetryDaemon` on DEBUG or higher, then reset your DB connection (just edit -> save with the same settings), you should see what we \u201cexpect\u201d the validation query, rate, etc, settings to be.", "score": 0.4651208, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://stackoverflow.com/questions/3668506/efficient-sql-test-query-or-validation-query-that-will-work-across-all-or-most", "title": "Efficient SQL test query or validation query that will work across all (or most ...", "content": "Many database connection pooling libraries provide the ability to test their SQL connections for idleness. For example, the JDBC pooling library c3p0", "score": 0.35682812, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://dba.stackexchange.com/questions/223425/how-to-test-query", "title": "sql server - how to test query", "content": "Still be very careful with this in production or shared dev/test/other instances though: long-running updates will still consume resources (IO, CPU, memory) so may slow down other users and locks will be held as needed so you can still cause the delays and other issues (potentially deadlocks) that your data changing statements normally would. To test the result you need not to `delete` smth at all,. While it can be not a problem to `begin tran`, do a `delete` and then `rollback` for a table with 10 rows in test environment, it can be a problem on large volumes of data or in concurrent environment, where you'll `lock` your table in absence of `index` on `colname`, and `delete` (and its `rollback`) will generate a lot of `transaction log` since `delete` is `fully logged operation` in any recovery model. **Reminder:** Answers generated by AI tools are not allowed due to Database Administrators Stack Exchange's artificial intelligence policy. Thanks for contributing an answer to Database Administrators Stack Exchange!", "score": 0.33770624, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm-explorations/test/latest/Test.Html.Query", "title": "Test.Html.Query - test 2.2.1", "content": "Translate a Html value into a Single query. This is how queries typically begin. import Html import Test.Html.Query as Query import Test exposing (test)", "score": 0.31675008, "raw_content": null}]}
